Notes: HF base with serial CAT (freq/mode/power/gain controllable). Baud approximate - confirm 9600 vs 4800 in manual; TTL-level jack, ERW-7 does level conversion.
Source: Hamlib rig backend rigs/alinco/dx77.c (rig caps)
Source Note: CAT params confirmed against the Hamlib dx77 backend: 9600 baud, 8 data bits, 2 STOP bits, no parity, no handshake (the stop-bits flag checks out - Alinco uses 2). No built-in sound card. The interface recommendation is the standard community setup (Digirig/SignaLink on mic+speaker); Digirig has no dedicated DX-77 cable page, and the DX-77's CAT is mainly freq/mode, so RTS/VOX is the dependable PTT.

Connection Method: CAT via the Alinco ERW-7 USB cable (or USB-serial) into the receiver's serial jack; for digital decode feed the audio/line output into a soundcard. Receive-only. Hamlib supports Alinco control.
Recommended Interface: ERW-7 / USB-serial on the CAT jack for control; a sound card (Digirig/SignaLink) on the line/speaker output for audio decode
PTT Method: n/a - receive only
Radio Menu Settings: Enable serial control; set 9600 8-N-2.
Notes: HF communications receiver (0.135-35 MHz) with computer control. RX only; baud approximate - confirm in manual.
Source: Hamlib rigs/alinco/dxsr8.c (rig caps) + DriveR8 (Nick Bailey) for DX-R8/SR8/SR9
Source Note: Confirmed 9600-8-N-2 (the DX-R8 shares the Alinco DX-SR8/R8 protocol family; Hamlib's DX-SR8 backend defaults to 9600 baud, 8 data, 2 stop, no parity). Receive-only, no sound card, no PTT. Default baud is 9600 (radio menu can select others).

Connection Method: CAT via the Alinco ERW-7 USB cable (or USB-serial) into the 3.5mm 'SP'/serial jack at 9600-N-2; for digital-mode audio add a Digirig Mobile on the mic/audio jacks (no built-in codec).
Recommended Interface: ERW-7 / USB-serial on the CAT jack for control; Digirig/SignaLink on the mic + speaker/data jacks for digital audio
PTT Method: Interface RTS keying (mic PTT line) or VOX; the DX-SR8 also accepts a CAT TX on/off command
Radio Menu Settings: Set serial to 9600, 8 data bits, No parity, 2 stop bits; connect to the SP port; adjust drive.
Notes: HF transceiver; confirmed 9600-N-2 CAT via ERW-7. Hamlib support is beta/limited - DriveR8 or maker sw also work. No built-in codec.
Source: Hamlib rig backend rigs/alinco/dxsr8.c (rig caps)
Source Note: Confirmed via the Hamlib dxsr8 backend: 9600 baud, 8 data bits, 2 STOP bits, no parity, no handshake (stop-bits flag verified = 2). No built-in sound card. Interface recommendation is the standard Digirig/SignaLink + ERW-7 setup.

Connection Method: CAT via the Alinco ERW-7 USB cable (or USB-serial) into the serial jack at 9600-N-2; audio/SDR via the radio's I/Q or AF output into a soundcard/Digirig (no built-in USB codec).
Recommended Interface: ERW-7 / USB-serial on the CAT jack; Digirig/SignaLink on mic + speaker for TX/RX audio; external sound card on the I/Q output for SDR RX / panadapter
PTT Method: Interface RTS keying (mic PTT line) or VOX; also a CAT TX on/off command
Radio Menu Settings: Set serial to 9600 8-N-2; enable I/Q output for SDR use; adjust drive.
Cable/Port: 3.5mm serial jack (CAT) + I/Q output + mic/audio jacks
Notes: SDR-capable HF transceiver: CAT like DX-SR8 plus an analog I/Q output for panadapter/SDR software. No built-in USB codec.
Source: Hamlib rigs/alinco/dxsr8.c (DX-SR8/SR9 share the protocol)
Source Note: Confirmed 9600-8-N-2 (the DX-SR9 shares the DX-SR8 protocol and Hamlib backend: 9600/8/2/none). The DX-SR9 adds a dedicated I/Q output for SDR RX/panadapter, but it still has no built-in sound card, so an external stereo sound card is needed on the I/Q output.

Bands: RX 30 kHz-30 MHz; TX HF ham bands
Connection Method: Single Mini-USB cable does everything: USB CAT (FT-817 protocol) plus a built-in USB Audio codec, so no external interface is needed.
CAT Protocol: NOT UHSDR. Repacked JUMA TRX2A - RS-232 CAT with selectable Yaesu FT-897 emulation OR Kenwood TS-480 emulation (plus native JUMA-TRX2 protocol)
Baud: Real RS-232 serial, baud DOES matter - default 9600, selectable 1200-115200 (framing 8-N-1). It is NOT 'ignored'.
Built-in Sound Card: No - no USB/USB-codec at all. RX I/Q is available on an output for SDR display (receive only); TX audio via the mic
Recommended Interface: USB-to-RS232 (true-level) serial cable for CAT + an external PC stereo sound card on the I/Q output for RX SDR (no I/Q input, so no SDR TX); mic path for TX audio
PTT Method: PTT via the Yaesu/Kenwood CAT TX command over the RS-232 link (a real RS-232 port, not a virtual COM)
Radio Menu Settings: Run UHSDR firmware 2.0.0+ (USB CAT + USB audio always enabled); select the virtual COM port and USB Audio device in your software
Notes: mcHF / RS-918 / UHSDR-family SDR QRP transceiver. Software: set rig = FT-817, baud irrelevant. Verify firmware supports USB audio (2.0.0+).
Source: QRPblog (HF One MKII = repacked JUMA TRX2A) + JUMA TRX2 Operating Manual
Source Note: Corrected. The 'HF One MKII' is a repackaged JUMA TRX2A (AD9851 DDS + PIC30F5014), NOT an mcHF/UHSDR radio, so the claimed 'FT-817 / UHSDR / baud-ignored / built-in USB audio' is wrong. Per the JUMA TRX2 manual it uses a real RS-232 port (3.5 mm, +/-RS-232 levels), default 9600-8-N-1 (1200-115200 selectable), with RS-232 modes: native JUMA, Yaesu CAT (emulates the FT-897), Kenwood TS-480 CAT, and Test. No USB and no built-in sound card; SDR use is via an I/Q output (RX only) into a PC sound card. Identification of HF One MKII = JUMA TRX2A per QRPblog; CAT params per the JUMA TRX2 Operating Manual.

Connection Method: Single USB-C cable does everything: it presents a virtual COM port with Kenwood TS-480 CAT emulation, and audio is streamed over that same CAT serial link (via the truSDX audio driver) - so no separate sound card is needed.
Flow Control: None (DTR must be HIGH; RTS keys PTT/CW)
Built-in Sound Card: Yes but non-standard - audio is streamed over the CAT USB serial (UA0/UA1/UA2), requiring the truSDX audio driver; it is NOT a USB-audio-class device
Recommended Interface: None - single USB-C cable using the truSDX audio-over-serial driver (CAT + audio on one port)
PTT Method: CAT (TS-480 TX command); the RTS line also keys PTT/CW
Radio Menu Settings: Set rig = Kenwood TS-480 in software; baud 115200 (fw 2.00t+); DTR HIGH, RTS low on RX; install the truSDX driver to split audio from CAT
Notes: QRP SDR transceiver. Works with WSJT-X/JS8Call/fldigi/Winlink as a TS-480. Note the audio-over-CAT quirk needs the special truSDX driver rather than a normal USB sound device.
Source: DL2MAN - (tr)uSDX Details
Source Note: Claim correct per DL2MAN: TS-480 subset, 38400 / 115200 (fw 2.00t+), 8N1, No flow control, DTR HIGH, RTS LOW on RX (HIGH to key CW/PTT). Audio is streamed over the serial link (UA commands) via the dedicated truSDX driver, not a standard USB sound card.

Connection Method: USB. Standalone SDR transceiver with two USB ports: a dedicated CAT serial port (FTDI) for rig control and a second USB for RX/TX audio/IQ to the FDM-SW2 software. Best setup = both USB cables to the PC.
CAT Protocol: ELAD CAT (custom command set, Kenwood/Elecraft-like; Hamlib 'ELAD FDM-DUO' backend)
Baud: 38400 default; CAT BAUD menu selectable (9600 / 19200 / 38400 / up to 115200)
Built-in Sound Card: Yes - USB RX-TX audio/IQ codec on a dedicated USB port (separate from the CAT USB port)
Recommended Interface: None needed - two USB-B ports (CAT via FTDI FT232R + digital audio) run direct to the PC
PTT Method: CAT command or software (FDM-SW2); DIGITAL-mode PTT via the virtual COM port
Radio Menu Settings: Menu [70] CAT BAUD = 38400 (match software), 8-N-1; select DIGITAL/mode as needed; install FTDI FT232R driver
Cable/Port: Two USB (CAT serial + RX/TX audio), plus optional standalone operation
Notes: Full HF+6m SDR transceiver usable standalone or PC-connected. CAT port appears as an FTDI virtual COM; audio is over USB, so no external sound-card interface is needed.
Source: ELAD FDM-DUO User Manual v2.6 (official)
Source Note: Official manual confirms the CAT USB port uses an FTDI FT232R and a separate USB port carries RX/TX digital audio (built-in codec). Hamlib has a dedicated FDM-DUO backend; default 38400, 8N1.

Connection Method: CAT via the optional KIO2 RS-232 module and a USB-serial adapter for rig control; a separate sound-card interface handles digital-mode audio (no built-in codec).
CAT Protocol: Elecraft / Kenwood-like (recognizes a subset of the Kenwood TS-570 command set)
Recommended Interface: KIO2 RS-232 (DB9) via a USB-to-RS232 adapter for CAT + Digirig Mobile (or SignaLink USB) for audio
PTT Method: CAT (software) or Digirig/SignaLink RTS/DTR keying into the PTT line
Radio Menu Settings: Install KIO2; in host software select K2 or Kenwood generic at 4800 8-N-1
Cable/Port: DB9 RS-232 (KIO2) + mic/accessory jack for audio
Notes: CAT requires the KIO2 option; without it there is no serial control. If software lacks a K2 profile, use TS-570/Kenwood-generic at 4800.
Source: Elecraft KIO2 RS-232 Serial Interface Manual (rev C)
Source Note: STOP BITS CORRECTED 1 -> 2. The Elecraft KIO2 (and the K2 firmware serial engine) run 4800 baud, 8 data bits, NO parity, 2 STOP bits, no handshaking. Confirmed in the KIO2 manual and multiple Elecraft sources. TS-570 command-subset claim confirmed.

Connection Method: CAT via the optional KIO2 RS-232 module and a USB-serial adapter; a separate sound-card interface handles digital-mode audio (no built-in codec).

Recommended Interface: RS-232 (DB9) for CAT - either the KPA100's built-in RS-232 or a KIO2 - via a USB-to-RS232 adapter + Digirig Mobile (or SignaLink USB) for audio

Radio Menu Settings: Install KIO2; select K2/Kenwood generic at 4800 8-N-1

Notes: 100W version of the K2; CAT identical to the K2 (needs KIO2). Use TS-570/Kenwood-generic at 4800 if no K2 profile.
Source: Elecraft KIO2 Manual (rev C) + KPA100/K2 serial notes
Source Note: STOP BITS CORRECTED 1 -> 2. On the K2/100 the KPA100 option provides the RS-232 port (so a KIO2 is not required), but it runs the same K2 serial firmware: 4800 baud, 8 data bits, no parity, 2 stop bits. TS-570 subset confirmed.

Connection Method: CAT via the rear RS-232 (DB9) port using a USB-to-serial adapter (or Elecraft KUSB); a separate sound-card interface handles digital audio (no built-in codec).
Recommended Interface: DB9 RS-232 for CAT (KIO3, or USB-to-serial / KUSB) + Digirig K3 cable for line audio and PTT (SignaLink USB as an audio-only alternative)

Cable/Port: DB9 RS-232 + rear ACC/line-in/out for audio
Notes: No internal sound codec; audio goes over the rear line jacks to a Digirig/SignaLink. Elecraft K3/KX driver in most logging/digital software.
Source: Elecraft K3S/K3/KX3/KX2 Programmer's Reference (Rev G4)
Source Note: Programmer's Reference confirms 8 data bits, no parity, 1 stop bit; baud 4800-38400 with 38400 the practical default. No built-in sound codec on the K3.

Connection Method: CAT via the rear USB (built-in USB-serial) or RS-232 port; a separate sound-card interface handles digital audio (no built-in mic codec).
Recommended Interface: Built-in USB (KIO3B, virtual COM) or USB-to-serial for CAT + Digirig Mobile for line audio (SignaLink USB as an audio-only alternative)
PTT Method: CAT (software) or Digirig PTT-line keying; RTTY/data PTT via ACC

Source Note: K3S adds a built-in USB port (KIO3B/FTDI) that provides a virtual COM for CAT but NO USB audio codec - audio is still external, so 'built-in soundcard = No' is correct. Serial 38400, 8N1.

Connection Method: Single USB cable: the K4 has built-in USB CAT plus a built-in USB audio codec, so rig control and digital-mode audio both run over one USB connection.

Built-in Sound Card: Yes - a single USB cable creates a USB AUDIO CODEC plus two FTDI virtual COM ports
Recommended Interface: None needed - single USB cable (audio codec + virtual serial ports)

Radio Menu Settings: Enable USB CAT/audio; select K4 (or K3 emulation) in software; set DATA/AFSK mode
Cable/Port: USB (single cable) or Ethernet for networked use
Notes: Modern direct-sampling SDR transceiver; one USB does control + audio. Also has Ethernet for remote operation.
Source: Elecraft K4 Programmer's Reference (Rev D4) / K4 Built-In Manual
Source Note: Confirmed: connecting the K4 by USB creates a USB AUDIO CODEC sound device and two virtual serial ports; the K4 executes a superset of K3/K3S commands (legacy K3 compatibility). 38400 8N1.

Connection Method: Single USB cable: the G59 has a built-in USB audio codec plus USB control, driven by GSDR/PowerSDR. CAT is via a virtual serial port (uses the SDR-1000/PowerSDR CAT ID).
CAT Protocol: Kenwood-like CAT via GSDR/PowerSDR virtual COM (rig control runs over the native USB-HID link)
Baud: n/a - control over USB-HID; CAT to third-party apps via virtual COM
Built-in Sound Card: No - USB is HID CONTROL only (VID 0xFFFE / PID 0x1970); I/Q audio requires an external/PC stereo sound card
Recommended Interface: Quality SDR-capable external stereo sound card for I/Q (line-in/out) + USB (HID) for control - NOT a single USB cable
PTT Method: Software (GSDR/PowerSDR) PTT; DIGU/DIGL for data
Radio Menu Settings: Install GSDR/PowerSDR; set DIGU/DIGL; configure virtual COM (SDR-1000 ID) and VAC
Notes: Multiband SDR transceiver kit with onboard USB codec, so one USB does control + audio. Use HRD/DM780 or WSJT-X via the virtual CAT/audio.
Source: Genesis G59 USB Control Protocol (wd8rde) + G59 Digital Modes Setup
Source Note: Corrected. The claim said the G59 has a built-in USB audio codec and single-USB-cable operation. In fact the G59's USB is a HID CONTROL interface only (reverse-engineered: VID 0xFFFE, PID 0x1970, 64-byte HID control blocks); I/Q audio still goes through an external PC/USB sound card, exactly like the G40/G3020. It is a 'soundcard-based SDR' with no onboard audio codec.

Connection Method: Single USB-C cable to the PC carries both CAT control and the radio's built-in USB sound card (digital modes) — one cable does everything
CAT Protocol: Yaesu CAT (emulates FT-817/FT-847; use Hamlib FT-817, or the newer GUOHETEC backend)
Baud: Config-free over USB-C CDC serial (FT-817 emulation; ~4800-38400 nominal, framing not enforced by USB)
Stop Bits: 2 (FT-817 nominal; not enforced over USB CDC, 1 also works)
Built-in Sound Card: Yes - USB sound codec carried on the same USB-C cable
Recommended Interface: None needed - single USB-C cable carries both CAT and audio
PTT Method: CAT / software PTT in data mode (WSJT-X etc. key via Hamlib CAT)
Radio Menu Settings: Set radio to USB mode for modulation data (SDR mode sends IQ); install the Windows USB-C serial driver; in Hamlib/WSJT-X select Yaesu FT-817/FT-847
Cable/Port: USB Type-C (one cable for CAT + audio)
Notes: Modern full-mode SDR manpack (100 kHz-2 GHz RX; HF/VHF/UHF ~20 W TX). CAT emulates FT-817/FT-847; one USB-C cable carries serial CAT + built-in sound card. FT8/RTTY/PSK direct. Also controllable via HAM-BOX app. Confirm exact baud in-app if a rate is prompted.
Source: GUOHETEC PMR-171 User Operation Manual (FCC filing)
Source Note: User manual confirms FT-817/FT-847 CAT emulation, that the USB-C cable integrates the sound-card function, and that baud need not be configured. FT-817's nominal serial framing is 8N2 but USB CDC does not enforce stop bits, so the claim's '2 (1 also works)' is accurate.

Notes: Compact HF/VHF/UHF all-mode SDR (RX 100 kHz-2 GHz; TX HF/50/70/144/430 MHz). Built-in sound card + Bluetooth + serial CAT. FT8 without any external interface; Hamlib via Guohe custom lib or FT-817 emulation.

Connection Method: Ethernet (RJ45) to the PC/LAN. The radio is controlled entirely by openHPSDR-protocol SDR software (SparkSDR, Quisk, PowerSDR/Thetis, piHPSDR) which streams IQ, audio, and control over UDP — no serial CAT cable or external sound card is used.
CAT Protocol: none native (openHPSDR/Hermes Protocol 1 over UDP); SDR app can expose a virtual serial CAT port for logging/WSJT-X
Built-in Sound Card: n/a — IQ/audio streamed over Ethernet (no analog audio, no USB codec)
Recommended Interface: None — direct Ethernet; use a wired, shielded CAT6/7 cable (never Wi-Fi)
PTT Method: Software PTT in the SDR app (virtual CAT/RTS to WSJT-X)
Radio Menu Settings: No on-device menus; set radio IP/gateware and sample rate in the SDR app
Notes: Open-hardware AD9866/HPSDR HF SDR transceiver. Digital modes via the SDR app's virtual audio + virtual CAT. Ethernet-only design.
Source: No CAT — audio-only digital via sound-card interface/VOX; frequency set manually (general amateur practice)

Connection Method: Standalone Raspberry Pi-based SDR with its own touchscreen and built-in sound card; runs FT8/digital modes natively on the radio. For PC control connect via Ethernet/Wi-Fi (SSH/VNC) or its Hamlib rigctld TCP interface — no external CAT cable or sound card needed.
CAT Protocol: Hamlib-compatible network CAT (NET rigctl over TCP); no serial CAT jack
Baud: n/a (network TCP; Hamlib default rigctld port 4532)
Built-in Sound Card: Yes — internal Raspberry Pi 4 audio codec; FT8/RTTY/PSK31/CW all run self-contained on the radio
Recommended Interface: None (self-contained; network/TCP control only)
PTT Method: On-screen / software T/R (internal); PTT over the network CAT
Radio Menu Settings: Configure via the on-screen sbitx software; set network/rigctld for remote control
Cable/Port: Ethernet / USB (Raspberry Pi); no analog audio cabling needed
Notes: Modern hybrid SDR transceiver kit built on a Raspberry Pi 4. Self-contained digital operation; PC integration is via network/Hamlib, not a serial CAT cable.
Source: HF Signals sBitx v2 product page
Source Note: Confirmed self-contained SDR on an internal Raspberry Pi 4 with integrated audio codec and all digital-mode software on-board; control is via a Hamlib-compatible network interface (TCP), no serial CAT jack. Port 4532 is the Hamlib default; self-contained use needs no external CAT at all.

Connection Method: USB (mini-USB on the Raduino/Arduino Nano) provides CAT via KD8CEC firmware emulating a Yaesu FT-817. Add a sound-card interface into the mic/speaker jacks for digital audio.
Recommended Interface: Digirig Mobile for audio (SignaLink alt) + the Raduino/Nano's USB for CAT
PTT Method: CAT (rig PTT) or VOX
Radio Menu Settings: None required; use CAT PTT; ensure KD8CEC firmware loaded (CH340 USB-serial driver)
Notes: Analog/hybrid HF transceiver kit. Stock v6 firmware has limited CAT; KD8CEC recommended. Set WSJT-X rig=FT-817, 38400 8-N-2, CAT PTT.
Source: KD8CEC uBITX firmware README + uBITX WSJT-X setup guides
Source Note: Verified: KD8CEC recommends Rig=FT-817, 38400 baud, 8 data bits, 2 STOP BITS, no parity, CAT-controlled PTT. The 2 stop bits is correct (Yaesu FT-817 CAT format) — confirmed, not a typo.

Connection Method: Two USB connections to the PC: (1) the onboard Arduino Nano USB-serial for frequency/band control, and (2) a stereo USB sound card carrying the IQ baseband to/from J4 (RX-OUT) and J5 (TX-IN). Runs under SDR software (HDSDR/Quisk/etc.) as a quadrature IQ SDR.
Built-in Sound Card: No — requires an external stereo 24-bit/96 ksps USB sound card for IQ (HobbyPCB recommends the StarTech ICUSBAUDIO2D)
Recommended Interface: External stereo USB sound card (StarTech ICUSBAUDIO2D, 24-bit/96 ksps) for IQ + the onboard USB-serial for control
PTT Method: Software T/R via the control USB (SDR app switches TX and the Si5351)
Radio Menu Settings: No on-device menus; set frequency/band over serial via the SDR/control app
Cable/Port: USB (Arduino Nano) for control + 3.5mm IQ in/out (J4/J5) to the sound card
Notes: Not a standalone radio — it is an IQ SDR front end. Needs a PC, a stereo sound card, and two USB ports.
Source: HobbyPCB RS-HFIQ Quick Start (ManualsLib) + RS-HFIQ technical site
Source Note: Confirmed 57600 baud, No parity, 1 stop bit (8 data bits), RTS/DTR HIGH; external StarTech ICUSBAUDIO2D 24-bit/96 ksps card digitizes the I/Q. Hamlib 'RS-HFIQ' backend exists.

Connection Method: CI-V computer control via the rear CI-V REMOTE jack. Requires a level converter — a USB CI-V cable (or Icom CT-17) — between radio and PC. Add a sound-card interface on the accessory/phone-patch jack for digital audio.

Radio Menu Settings: CI-V address fixed at 0x24; CI-V baud set by internal jumper (try 1200); transceive as needed

Connection Method: Professional marine HF receivers with factory serial/remote control (often RS-422/NBDP-oriented); for typical use tune manually and decode from the audio out.
Recommended Interface: Factory remote interface if fitted; else sound-card interface on audio out
PTT Method: n/a (receiver)
Radio Menu Settings: Configure factory remote if installed; else tune manually
Notes: Coast-station receivers; remote control is installation-specific (RS-422/serial). Uncertain.
Source: No authoritative remote-spec source found (JRC commercial receiver)

Connection Method: QRP HF kit with an RS-232 serial port (firmware plus a basic remote command set); connect via USB-serial. For digital modes use a Digirig into the audio/key jacks. Frequency mostly set manually.
CAT Protocol: JUMA native serial; also selectable Yaesu-CAT emulation mode for logging/PC software
Recommended Interface: USB-serial for CAT/firmware; external audio interface (Digirig Mobile or SignaLink) on the audio jacks for digital modes
PTT Method: VOX, external-interface keying, or CAT PTT when in Yaesu-emulation mode
Radio Menu Settings: Set serial baud (19200, verify); enable remote if used
Notes: All-band (TRX-2A)/80-40m (TRX-2) QRP kit; serial supports firmware and a limited remote command set, not a mainstream CAT. Baud/support uncertain.
Source: JUMA TRX2 Operating Manual
Source Note: BAUD CORRECTED: claimed '19200 (verify)' — the operating manual sets the RS-232 port default to 9600 baud, 8N1. CAT protocol note added: the TRX-2 serial port has two modes (native JUMA-TRX2 protocol for the PA100D amp, and a Yaesu-CAT emulation for PC logging). 8/1/None confirmed.

Connection Method: PC-controlled by design: connect the rear RS-232 port to the PC (USB-serial) and run KC505/kcat/Hamlib. Serial control is required (no front panel). Use the rear audio in/out for digital modes.
Recommended Interface: USB-serial (required — the radio is PC-controlled) for control; Digirig / SignaLink on the rear audio in/out jacks for digital modes
PTT Method: Software/CAT PTT via the serial control protocol (Digirig RTS on rear audio jacks as alternate)
Radio Menu Settings: None on-radio; configure COM port in KC505/kcat at 9600 8-N-1
Notes: Headless PC-controlled HF DSP transceiver; RS-232 @9600 mandatory. Requires polling to keep the port open. Hamlib-supported.
Source: Hamlib Kachina 505DSP rig capabilities
Source Note: Hamlib 505dsp caps confirm 9600/9600 baud, 8 data, 1 stop, no parity, no handshake. All claimed fields correct. (The 505DSP has no front panel — a PC over serial is mandatory.)

Connection Method: Single micro-USB cable to PC provides a virtual COM port for CAT and the built-in KISS TNC (APRS/packet); Hamlib 'Kenwood TH-D72'. Set frequency by CAT or manually. For soundcard digital voice you'd add an external audio interface.
Built-in Sound Card: No (built-in 1200/9600 KISS TNC + mini-USB virtual COM, not a PC audio codec)
Recommended Interface: None for CAT/TNC (built-in mini-USB virtual COM port); add an external audio interface only if sound-card modes are needed
PTT Method: CAT/data (TNC); PTT via data
Radio Menu Settings: Enable PC interface; set COM speed 9600; TNC/KISS as needed
Notes: APRS HT with built-in TNC and USB virtual COM; Hamlib thd72. Confirmed 9600 default.
Source: Kenwood TH-D72A instruction manual / Kenwood HT command reference
Source Note: Kenwood HT command set at 9600 8N1. Built-in mini-USB serial + 1200/9600 TNC; no USB audio codec (unlike the D74/D75). All claimed fields correct.

Connection Method: Single micro-USB cable: PC sees a virtual COM port (CAT + KISS TNC) AND a USB Audio device for digital modes. Install the Kenwood VCP driver; Hamlib 'Kenwood TH-D74'. Set frequency by CAT or manually.
Recommended Interface: None (a single micro-USB provides CAT + USB audio + KISS TNC)
PTT Method: CAT/data PTT; or data-mode via USB
Radio Menu Settings: Install Kenwood VCP driver; enable PC interface; set COM 9600; USB audio on
Notes: D-STAR + APRS HT; micro-USB gives virtual COM for CAT/KISS plus a USB audio codec, so no external interface needed. Hamlib thd74.
Source: Kenwood TH-D74A User Manual (USB / USB Audio Output Level, p.97)
Source Note: Confirmed: the TH-D74 exposes a USB audio device — the user manual has a 'Setting USB Audio Output Level' menu (p.97), and the micro-USB carries CAT, USB audio, and the KISS TNC. 9600 8N1 CAT. All claimed fields correct.

Connection Method: Single USB-C cable: PC sees a virtual COM port (CAT + KISS TNC) AND a USB Audio device for digital modes. Hamlib 'Kenwood TH-D75'. Set frequency by CAT or manually.
Recommended Interface: None (a single USB-C provides serial CAT + USB audio in/out + KISS TNC, plus charging)
PTT Method: CAT/data PTT; or data-mode via USB
Radio Menu Settings: Enable PC interface; set COM 9600; USB audio on; TNC/KISS as needed
Notes: Newest tri-band D-STAR/APRS HT; USB-C provides virtual COM for CAT/KISS plus USB audio, so no external interface needed. Hamlib thd75.
Source: Kenwood USA TH-D75A product page
Source Note: Confirmed via Kenwood: the TH-D75A USB-C port carries serial communications, audio in/out and charging (built-in USB sound card). 9600 8N1 CAT, built-in KISS TNC. All claimed fields correct.

Connection Method: Connect the radio's serial data jack to a PC COM port (Kenwood PG-4W-type / homebrew cable, or a USB-serial adapter); Hamlib 'Kenwood TH-D7'. Built-in TNC for APRS/packet. Set frequency by CAT or manually.
Built-in Sound Card: No (built-in 1200/9600 TNC, not a PC audio codec)
Recommended Interface: USB-serial / level-converter cable to the radio's 2.5mm data jack for CAT + TNC; no external audio interface needed for TNC modes
PTT Method: CAT/data (TNC); PTT via data
Radio Menu Settings: Set COM speed (9600); enable TNC/PC mode
Notes: APRS HT with built-in TNC; serial (RS-232-level) data port, not USB. Hamlib thd7. Verify cable level conversion.
Source: Kenwood TH-D7A/E instruction manual / Kenwood HT command reference
Source Note: Kenwood HT command set at 9600 8N1 over the 2.5mm serial data jack (needs a level-converter/USB-serial cable — the radio has no native USB). Built-in 1200/9600 TNC, no USB audio codec. Claimed fields correct.

Connection Method: Connect the radio's data jack to a PC COM port via a Kenwood/homebrew programming-control cable (or USB-serial); Hamlib 'Kenwood TH-F7' family supports rig control. Set frequency by CAT or manually. Digital via a Digirig on the mic/earphone jacks.

Source Note: The TH-F6A/TH-F7E Protocol Specification states the serial parameters are '9600, 8, 1, none' with no other speeds available. All claimed fields correct.

Connection Method: Live CAT/programming over the operation-panel PC port (mini-DIN) using a Kenwood PG-5G (or USB-equivalent) serial cable; APRS/packet uses the built-in TNC, or add a Digirig on the 6-pin DATA jack for sound-card digital modes.
Recommended Interface: Kenwood PG-5G (or USB clone) for CAT + Digirig Mobile on the 6-pin DATA jack for audio (SignaLink alt.)
PTT Method: CAT / built-in TNC (or Digirig RTS on DATA jack)
Radio Menu Settings: Set PC-port COM baud (menu 1-9-5, default 9600); DATA-jack packet speed 1200/9600 as needed.
Cable/Port: Operation-panel PC port (mini-DIN → DB9) for CAT + 6-pin mini-DIN DATA jack for packet audio
Notes: 2m/70cm APRS mobile with built-in TNC. Hamlib model 'TM-D700'. Programming via MCP / RT Systems / CHIRP.
Source: Hamlib rig capabilities (tmd700.c)
Source Note: Confirmed against Hamlib tmd700 backend: 9600-57600 baud (default 9600), 8/1/None, hardware handshake. Built-in TNC (not a PC audio codec). Interface uses hardware flow control (RTS/CTS).

Connection Method: Single USB-C cable to the PC (also Bluetooth / wireless LAN); the built-in KISS TNC handles APRS/packet (1200/9600) and the built-in MMDVM gives D-STAR/reflector access from the PC.

The claimed 'KISS TNC / MMDVM over USB-C' is accurate; clarified that this is packet/D-STAR data, not an HF-style USB audio codec.

The TS-2000/TS-B2000 DOES have TX;/RX; CAT commands, but sending a CAT PTT always mutes the ACC-2 (data) audio and switches to the front MIC input, so it cannot key digital audio. Data ops therefore use hardware PTT (ACC-2 PKS pin) or interface RTS/DTR, exactly as claimed. Default COM baud is 9600 (menu 56).

Radio Menu Settings: Set CAT to TS-2000 (or native Lab599) at 9600 8-N-1; set ACC audio in/out levels. Digirig must be Rev 1.6+ preset for TX-500.
Cable/Port: GX12 ACC (serial + audio) → Digirig; single USB to PC
Notes: Portable SDR HF/50MHz rig with no internal USB codec, so the Digirig (or equivalent) supplies audio. In software select RIG type TS-2000.
Source: Lab599 Discovery TX-500 User Manual v03.2021
Source Note: Confirmed. TX-500 manual states the CAT interface is compatible with the KENWOOD (TS-2000) command set at 9600 8-N-1; no built-in codec, so an external sound-card interface (Digirig TX-500 cable) is required.

Connection Method: Single USB cable (Mini-USB): UHSDR firmware provides CAT (Yaesu FT-817 emulation) over a virtual COM port plus USB audio I/O on the same cable.
Baud: Any (USB CDC virtual COM - baud is ignored)
Built-in Sound Card: Yes (UHSDR USB audio, 48 kHz 16-bit; enable 'USB CAT and Audio' mode)
Recommended Interface: None (single USB cable provides CAT + audio)
PTT Method: CAT (FT-817 TX command); DTR can key CW
Radio Menu Settings: In UHSDR set USB mode to CAT+Audio (VirtualCom); host software RIG = Yaesu FT-817, PTT = CAT, baud = any.
Notes: Open-source SDR QRP rig; with UHSDR firmware it is effectively single-cable like a modern rig. Line in/out jacks also exist as an analog alternative.
Source: UHSDR Wiki - USB CAT and AUDIO Mode
Source Note: Confirmed. UHSDR firmware exposes an emulated FT-817 CAT COM port plus an integrated 48 kHz USB Audio interface over the single USB link; PTT via the FT-817 CAT protocol.

Connection Method: CAT control via a USB-serial link emulating a Kenwood TS-480 (WSJT-X/Hamlib); digital-mode audio goes through a sound-card interface into the 3.5mm mic/speaker jacks. Newer (tr)uSDX / uSDX+ V2 clones add a built-in USB sound card so a single USB cable carries both CAT and audio.
Flow Control: None (DTR held HIGH; RTS HIGH keys PTT/CW)
Built-in Sound Card: No on the original PE1NNZ QCX-SSB (audio via 3.5mm jacks); Yes on codec-equipped (tr)uSDX / uSDX+ V2 clones
Recommended Interface: Digirig Mobile for audio (+ USB-serial for CAT if the board has no USB) — or None/single-USB on codec-equipped (tr)uSDX/uSDX+ variants
PTT Method: CAT PTT via RTS (RTS LOW on RX, HIGH keys TX/CW), or VOX
Radio Menu Settings: Match CAT baud to the host; in WSJT-X choose Kenwood TS-480, the serial port, PTT=CAT or RTS. Set audio in/out levels.
Cable/Port: 3.5mm mic/speaker jacks + serial/USB CAT (varies by board)
Notes: Open-source QRP SDR (PE1NNZ QCX-SSB firmware); many hardware variants. Connectivity depends on the specific board — some expose USB, some need an external FTDI. HF single- or multi-band.
Source: threeme3/usdx firmware (usdx.ino) + DL2MAN (tr)uSDX details
Source Note: Confirmed Kenwood TS-480 subset, 8-N-1, no flow control, DTR HIGH, RTS LOW on RX / HIGH to key PTT/CW. Refinement: 38400 is for older firmware; firmware 2.00t and up requires 115200.

Connection Method: Single USB cable to the PC — the kit has a built-in 96 kHz I/Q USB sound card and a DG8SAQ/PE0FKO Si570 USB control interface; run it in HDSDR/PowerSDR.
CAT Protocol: DG8SAQ/PE0FKO Si570 USB control (handled inside the SDR program via USB HID, not a serial CAT)
Built-in Sound Card: Yes — built-in full-speed USB I/Q sound card (appears as a standard USB audio device; no external card needed)
Recommended Interface: None (single USB cable for audio + control)
PTT Method: SDR software PTT via the USB control interface (DG8SAQ command 0x50), or VOX
Radio Menu Settings: No radio menus — in the SDR app select the Peaberry USB audio device for I/Q and the Si570/DG8SAQ interface for tuning; set band per the installed band-pair.
Cable/Port: USB Type-B (single cable: Si570 control + I/Q audio)
Notes: HF I/Q SDR transceiver kit (band-pair options 160-10m). Everything runs through the SDR software (HDSDR/PowerSDR); DG8SAQ/PE0FKO-compatible so widely supported.
Source: AE9RB Peaberry SDR V2 (Full) manual
Source Note: Confirmed: single full-speed USB for both audio and control; enumerates as a USB sound card (no add-on card). Control is the DG8SAQ/PE0FKO Si570 USB command set (PTT = command 0x50). Uses an Si570 synthesizer.

Connection Method: Single USB cable to the PC — built-in 48 kHz 24-bit USB sound card plus CAT (Kenwood TS-480/TS-440 subset). Run WSJT-X or similar; no external interface needed.

Built-in Sound Card: Yes — built-in 24-bit 48 ksps USB sound card
Recommended Interface: None (single USB cable for CAT + digital audio)
PTT Method: CAT PTT over USB (WSJT-X 'CAT'); also AUX PTT outputs (fw 1.05+)
Radio Menu Settings: Set band on multi-band builds; in software pick radio 'Kenwood TS-480', the QDX serial port and its USB audio device; set TX drive/level.
Cable/Port: USB (Type-B / Type-C by revision) — single cable
Notes: Digi-modes-only (FT8/etc.) SDR transceiver. Low-band (80/60/40/30/20m) or high-band (20/17/15/12/11/10m) build. CAT + audio over one USB cable.
Source: QRP Labs QDX product page
Source Note: QRP Labs confirms built-in 24-bit 48 ksps USB sound card, a USB virtual COM for CAT (Kenwood TS-440/TS-480 emulation — fldigi uses 'Kenwood TS440S'), baud irrelevant, 8-N-1, CAT PTT.

Connection Method: Single USB cable to the radio's USB (DFU) port: UHSDR firmware provides FT-817-compatible CAT plus USB audio/IQ over the one cable.
Baud: USB CDC virtual serial - baud rate is ignored by UHSDR (any value works; 38400 commonly set)
Built-in Sound Card: Yes - UHSDR USB audio codec (48 kHz / 16-bit) over the same Mini-USB port
Recommended Interface: None - single USB cable carries FT-817 CAT + USB audio; dedicated LINE in/out + PTT jacks also present for a SignaLink if preferred
PTT Method: CAT PTT (FT-817 TX on/off) or UHSDR virtual RTS (RTS = PTT, DTR = CW key)
Radio Menu Settings: UHSDR menu #60 (CAT mode / USB audio), enable Virtual RTS for RTS/DTR keying
Notes: mcHF SDR clone running UHSDR firmware; select 'Yaesu FT-817' (or FT-897) rig in Hamlib/WSJT-X.
Source: UHSDR Wiki - USB CAT and AUDIO Mode (df8oe)
Source Note: Claim correct. mcHF clone running UHSDR: FT-817 CAT emulation with baud ignored, plus a built-in USB audio codec (48k/16-bit) over the single Mini-USB (UHSDR 'USB CAT and AUDIO' mode). PTT via CAT or virtual RTS, CW via DTR.

Connection Method: Best via the Xiegu DE-19 data interface: one USB cable to the PC gives USB audio + CAT + PTT. CAT emulates Icom IC-7000 (CI-V). Alternatively a Digirig with the Xiegu 3.5 mm cable set.
Built-in Sound Card: No (the DE-19 provides the USB audio codec)
Recommended Interface: Xiegu DE-19 data interface (USB audio + CAT + PTT); or Digirig Mobile with the Xiegu 3.5 mm cable set
PTT Method: CAT PTT (RTS/DTR) or via the DE-19
Radio Menu Settings: Set the CAT/serial baud to match (19200); in software select Icom IC-7000, CI-V addr 0x70, RTS/DTR LOW.
Cable/Port: 3.5 mm CAT/serial jack + 3.5 mm audio (or a single DE-19 box)
Notes: QRP SDR HF transceiver. No built-in USB codec — the DE-19 (or a Digirig) supplies audio; CAT emulates Icom IC-7000.
Source: Hamlib xiegu.c + Xiegu G106 User Manual v1.31
Source Note: Confirmed. Hamlib's xiegu.c derives the G106 from IC-7000 caps with default CI-V address 0x70; the G106 manual documents CI-V at 19200 8-N-1. Some ops find IC-7100 emulation more reliable than IC-7000.

Connection Method: CAT via the 3.5 mm serial port (emulates Icom IC-7000, CI-V); audio via a separate 3.5 mm jack. Easiest is a Digirig Mobile with the Xiegu cable set (or Xiegu DE-19).
Built-in Sound Card: No (audio via 3.5 mm to a sound-card interface / DE-19)
Recommended Interface: Digirig Mobile with the Xiegu 3.5 mm cable set (serial to CAT port, audio to the audio jack); or Xiegu DE-19
PTT Method: CAT PTT (RTS/DTR) or interface PTT
Radio Menu Settings: Set CAT baud to 19200; in software select Icom IC-7000 (or IC-7100), CI-V addr 0x70, RTS/DTR LOW.
Cable/Port: 3.5 mm CAT/serial jack + 3.5 mm audio jack
Notes: 4-band QRP SDR HF transceiver. No built-in USB codec; CAT emulates Icom IC-7000. Not in Hamlib by name — IC-7000/IC-7100 profile at 19200 works.
Source: Hamlib xiegu.c + marxy G1M/Digirig writeup
Source Note: Confirmed. Xiegu CAT (Hamlib xiegu.c, IC-7000 based, serial_rate_max 19200, addr 0x70). FLrig users must set 19200, 1 stop bit and enable Echo. IC-7100 emulation is often more reliable than IC-7000 for the G1M.

Recommended Interface: CAT via the radio's built-in USB (CAT-only) OR the RS-232C port; audio via Digirig Mobile on the rear DATA/RTTY jack (SignaLink alt.); Yaesu SCU-17 is the OEM option that does both CAT + audio
PTT Method: CAT command, or interface RTS / DATA-jack PTT
Radio Menu Settings: Menu 039 CAT RATE=4800 (or 38400); menu 038 CAT RTS ON if using external control; select DATA/PKT mode; set data drive for clean ALC.
Cable/Port: USB Type-B (CAT only) + rear DATA/PKT mini-DIN (audio)
Notes: USB port is CAT-only; the radio has NO built-in sound card, so audio needs the SCU-17 or a Digirig on the DATA jack. Hamlib 'FTDX-1200'.

Source Note: Stop bits 1 -> 2: Hamlib ft1200 comment explicitly says 2 stop bits were 'found by testing with remote serial port' - empirical confirmation the FTDX1200 actually uses 8-N-2. Built-in soundcard 'No' confirmed (unlike the FTDX3000, the FTDX1200's USB is CAT-only; needs external audio).

Connection Method: Single USB-C cable to the PC (built-in USB CAT + USB Audio CODEC); rig control and digital-mode audio both over the one cable
Baud: 38400 (CAT-1 Enhanced / CAT-3 ports); 4800 fixed on CAT-2 Standard port
Stop Bits: 1 (selectable 1 or 2 on CAT-1/CAT-3; CAT-2 fixed at 1)
Built-in Sound Card: Yes (built-in USB Audio CODEC / USB-to-dual-UART bridge; USB-C carries CAT, audio in/out and TX control)
Recommended Interface: None (single USB-C cable)
PTT Method: CAT (USB), or RTS/DTR on the Standard (CAT-2) COM port
Radio Menu Settings: Set CAT RATE=38400; select DATA/PSK mode; set USB audio in/out levels for no ALC. Two virtual COM ports (CAT-1 Enhanced, CAT-2 Standard).
Notes: 2025 SDR HF/VHF/UHF base/portable flagship. Confirmed single USB-C for both CAT and audio. Exact default baud may vary by firmware; verify in the CAT reference manual.
Source: Yaesu FTX-1 Series Instruction Manual (CAT communication spec) + DX Engineering FTX-1 Optima product page
Source Note: PTT port assignment was reversed: per the FTX-1 CAT manual the Standard port (CAT-2) handles TX controls (PTT/CW/digital) and RTS/DTR PTT, while the Enhanced port (CAT-1) does CAT frequency/mode. Baud clarified (38400 on CAT-1/CAT-3, 4800 fixed on CAT-2). Stop bits are selectable 1 or 2 on CAT-1/CAT-3 (claim '1' is the usual default). Built-in codec and single-USB-C confirmed.
